status_notes: Initial research from Azure Monitor documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live Azure environment).
notes: Foundation monitoring and logging service. Includes Activity Log (control plane), Diagnostic Settings, Log Analytics, and Alerts.

data.description: Subscription-level log that records all Azure Resource Manager operations (control plane). Records who did what, when, and the result for resource creation, modification, deletion, and role assignments. Automatically collected for all Azure subscriptions. Retained for 90 days (free). Must be exported via diagnostic settings for longer retention or advanced analysis.

options.description: Resource Manager CRUD operations (create, update, delete, action)
Microsoft Defender for Cloud alerts and recommendations
Azure service health incidents, maintenance, and advisories
Azure Monitor alert activations
Azure Advisor recommendations
Azure Policy evaluation results
Autoscale engine events
Resource availability changes

default.note: Activity Log is automatically collected for 90 days. No configuration needed for basic access. Diagnostic settings required for export.

data.vendor_recommendation: Export Activity Log to a Log Analytics workspace for long-term retention and KQL-based querying. Set up alerts for critical operations (role assignments, resource deletions, policy changes).

configure.note: Activity Log is always on. 'Configure' means setting up export via diagnostic settings for retention beyond 90 days.

match_nodes.description: Route platform logs and metrics to destinations

data.description: Configure where Azure platform logs and metrics are sent. Each Azure resource can have multiple diagnostic settings routing different log categories to different destinations. Required for persisting resource logs beyond their default retention period.

options.description: Enables KQL queries, alerts, workbooks, and cross-resource analysis. Most common destination.
Cost-effective archival. Logs stored as JSON blobs.
Stream to external SIEM or custom processing pipeline.
Send to integrated partner monitoring solutions (Datadog, Elastic, etc.)

default.note: No diagnostic settings configured by default. Resource logs are not persisted unless diagnostic settings are created.

data.vendor_recommendation: Create diagnostic settings for all Azure resources that produce security-relevant logs. Send to a centralized Log Analytics workspace. Use Azure Policy to enforce diagnostic settings at scale.

data.description: Centralized log aggregation and analysis service. Uses Kusto Query Language (KQL) for powerful querying across all collected data. Data sources include Azure platform logs, Azure Monitor Agent (VM logs), Application Insights, and custom sources. Supports cross-workspace and cross-resource queries.

options.description: How long data is retained in the workspace
Volume discount pricing tiers
Filter and transform data before ingestion to reduce costs

data.vendor_recommendation: Create a centralized Log Analytics workspace for security logs. Use data collection rules to filter out noise before ingestion. Set appropriate retention periods per table. Use basic logs tier for high-volume, infrequently queried data.

match_nodes.description: Automated alerting on metrics and logs

data.description: Automated alerting system that fires notifications and actions based on conditions in metrics, logs, or activity log events. Alerts use action groups to define what happens when triggered (email, SMS, webhook, Logic App, ITSM, Azure Function, Event Hub).

options.description: Fire when a metric crosses a threshold. Near real-time (1-5 min). Examples: CPU > 90%, request latency > 500ms.
Fire when a KQL query against Log Analytics returns results. Configurable frequency. Examples: more than 5 failed sign-ins, unauthorized API calls.
Fire on specific Activity Log events. Real-time. Examples: resource deleted, role assigned, service health incident.
ML-based anomaly detection on Application Insights data. Automatic, no configuration needed.

data.vendor_recommendation: Create activity log alerts for critical operations (role assignments to Global Administrator, resource group deletions). Create log alerts for security events (failed sign-ins, suspicious activity). Use action groups to route to your ITSM and on-call systems.

configure.cli_metric: az monitor metrics alert create --name {name} --resource-group {rg} --scopes {resource-id} --condition 'avg Percentage CPU > 90' --action {action-group-id}
configure.cli_activity: az monitor activity-log alert create --name {name} --resource-group {rg} --condition category=Administrative and operationName=Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write --action-group {action-group-id}

match_nodes.description: Cloud security posture management and threat protection

data.description: Cloud security posture management (CSPM) and cloud workload protection platform (CWPP). Finds misconfigurations, provides security recommendations, detects threats, and protects workloads. Free tier provides basic CSPM (security recommendations, Secure Score). Paid Defender plans provide per-resource advanced threat detection and protection.

options.description: Security recommendations, Secure Score, Azure security benchmark assessment. Always available.
Advanced CSPM: attack path analysis, cloud security graph, agentless scanning, governance rules.
Threat detection for VMs, vulnerability assessment, just-in-time VM access, adaptive application controls.
Detect unusual access patterns, malware uploads, sensitive data exposure.
SQL threat detection, vulnerability assessment for databases.
Detect unusual Key Vault access patterns.
Runtime threat detection, vulnerability assessment for container images.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable at minimum the free tier for all subscriptions. Enable Defender plans for production workloads. Review Secure Score regularly and remediate high-severity recommendations.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Defender for Cloud

configure.cli: az security pricing create --name VirtualMachines --tier Standard
configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → {subscription} → Defender plans

data.description: Cloud-native Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) and Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response (SOAR). Built on Log Analytics. Collects data from Azure services, on-premises, and multi-cloud via data connectors. Provides threat detection via analytics rules, automated investigation and response via playbooks (Logic Apps), and hunting via KQL queries and notebooks.

options.description: 200+ connectors for Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, GCP, third-party services
Scheduled and near-real-time rules for threat detection
Correlated alerts grouped into incidents for investigation
Automated response workflows using Logic Apps
Proactive threat hunting with KQL queries, bookmarks, and Jupyter notebooks
Interactive dashboards and reports for security data visualization

data.vendor_recommendation: Deploy Microsoft Sentinel for organizations requiring SIEM capabilities. Connect all Azure and Microsoft 365 data sources. Start with built-in analytics rules and customize for your environment. Use automation rules and playbooks for common incident response actions.

configure.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Sentinel → {workspace} → Data connectors
